extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds. Analysis: (1) The only transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (on send_attempt if not opted_out), which is guarded by not opted_out — so sent can only become true when opted_out is false. (2) opted_out is monotonic: the only transition setting opted_out:=true is t1, and no transition ever sets opted_out back to false. (3) The transition that makes opted_out true, t1, atomically also sets sent:=false. Therefore at the instant opted_out becomes true, sent is false. (4) Once opted_out=true, t2 (the sole sent:=true transition) is permanently disabled because its guard not opted_out can never again hold. The transitions that can fire while opted_out=true are t1 (sent:=false), t3 (sent:=false), and t4 (sent:=false) — all of which keep sent false. Hence in every reachable state with opted_out=true, sent is false. While opted_out=false the implication is vacuously satisfied. No event sequence (within 16 events or any length) can reach opted_out=true with sent=true, so the property holds.
